mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive financial conditions dominate earnings expectations and discount-rate effects, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the short signal Sharpe exceeds 0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high consumer price inflation and elevated policy rates jointly increase real discount rates and signal an aggressive Federal Reserve, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales expansion predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate is below 4.0%, because tight labor markets validate demand as income-driven and sustainable rather than credit-fueled and fragile.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.0%, positive RSAFS growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production captures real-time manufacturing momentum that translates into positive near-term earnings revisions and 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.4 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration and impending earnings contraction, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: After the UNRATE trigger, SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of episodes, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and negative earnings surprises, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: After a >2% RSAFS decline, SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.4 million units signal durable construction demand and positive household formation, predicting positive 60-day S&P 500 returns through housing-sensitive sector earnings.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1400, SPX 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of triggered quarters over a 3-year rolling window.
